content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 20, 2006

Larry Minor
Agri-Empire
P.O. Box 490
San Jacinto, CA 92581

RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-06-052

Dear Mr. Minor:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Since your request seeks
general guidance, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.²

QUESTION

Does the Act prohibit you from holding two public offices concurrently?

CONCLUSION

There is nothing in the Act that prohibits a local public official, such as you, from
holding two public offices concurrently. However, other laws outside the jurisdiction of
the Fair Political Practices Commission ("Commission") may restrict a public official
from holding two public offices simultaneously, if those offices are determined to be
"incompatible" offices.

FACTS

You currently hold a position as a Board Member of the Valley Wide Recreation
and Park District. You are contemplating running for a position as a Board Member of
the Lake Hemet Municipal Water District. You ask whether the Act prohibits you from
holding two public offices concurrently.

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3).)

File No. I-06-052
Page No. 2


ANALYSIS

There is nothing in the Act that prohibits a local public official, such as you, from holding two public positions concurrently. Holding a position as a Board Member of the Valley Wide Recreation and Park District while also holding a position as a Board Member of the Lake Hemet Municipal Water District does not in itself, create a potential disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act. (See e.g. Johnson Advice Letter, No. I-04-244; Hernandez Advice Letter, No. I-04-240; and Foster Advice Letter, No. A-02-125.)

However, other laws outside the jurisdiction of the Commission may restrict a public official from holding two public offices simultaneously, if those offices are determined to be "incompatible" offices. You are urged to check with the legal counsel retained by your special district to determine if there are other laws that may be applicable, in light of the facts you present, that may cause a problem under the doctrine of incompatible offices. The Commission's advice is limited to matters arising under the Act. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(d).)

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.


Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

[signature]
By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division
